name: ai-streaming-text
title: AI Streaming Text
description: Incrementally reveals caller-supplied response text with a stable reading order, reduced-motion fallback, and caller-owned complete, cancel, and replace.

props.name: text
props.description: The text known so far. Append to it to stream; replace it to restart.

props.name: streaming
props.description: While true the text is revealed incrementally. Flip to false to complete or cancel.

props.name: speed
props.description: Reveal rate in characters per second. Ignored under prefers-reduced-motion.

props.name: onComplete
props.description: Fires once when the reveal catches up with `text`.

props.name: cursor
props.description: Blinking caret while text is still being revealed.

clinicalNotes: A half-rendered sentence is a clinically dangerous object: "no penicillin allergy" and "no penicillin" are the same string one word apart. So the reveal is character-order-stable and never reorders or rewrites what is already on screen, and a replacement stream keeps any shared prefix instead of flickering — a clinician who has read the first line can trust that line will not change under them. The animating copy is hidden from assistive technology and the completed text is announced once, in a polite live region: a per-character live region would re-announce the whole paragraph on every tick, which in practice means a screen-reader user never hears a clean sentence at all. Under prefers-reduced-motion the full text renders instantly — motion is decoration here, the content is the point. Completion state is caller-owned: the component reveals text, it does not decide when a response is finished, and it makes no claim that a finished response is a correct one.
notes: Primitives only — no markdown parsing, no transport. Ticks at 40ms and derives chars-per-tick from `speed`; the interval is not recreated per character.
